1t appears that the trouble be-
tween the Cement workers and the
Hodcarriers' union over the divis-
ion of work is not confined to Spo-
kane. At the last meeting of the
American Federation of Labor the
International Hodcarriers COM
planned of the entertainments of
the other organizations on work
over which they had undisputed
control for years. The federation
induced the international unions to
hold a meeting at which represen-
tative of the American Federation
of Labor would be present to set
tie the jurisdiction question. The
committee will meet in the near
future and it Is likely that further
trouble will be avoided by a mutual
understanding.


The report Of the session of the
American Federation of Labor
shows that Delegate McCallum of
Federal union, No. 11,624, Spokane,
offered a resolution, which 1t had
been adopted would have made 1t
impossible for the federation to
charter more than one Federal
union in any city with a population
of less than 100000. The resoiu-
ton had for its object the with
drawal of the charter of Federal
union No. 1922 and the consolida-
ton Of that union with Fderal No.
1924. The resolution was referred
to a 0ommittee, which reported
unfavorably.
